name: Deribit Crypto Derivatives Exchange API
what_it_does: Cryptocurrency derivatives exchange REST and WebSocket API focused on BTC and ETH options and perpetual futures — enabling professional traders and algorithmic trading systems to access options (calls/puts), perpetual swaps, and futures contracts with industry-leading liquidity for crypto options, with JSON-RPC over WebSocket as primary interface, comprehensive options analytics (greeks, IV surface), and institutional-grade derivatives infrastructure.
use_cases: Trading BTC/ETH options via Deribit JSON-RPC API for volatility and hedging strategies
Managing perpetual swap positions via Deribit API for directional crypto exposure
Accessing options analytics (delta, gamma, vega, IV) via Deribit API for options pricing models
Streaming options order book via Deribit WebSocket for market-making bots
Building crypto volatility surface models via Deribit API for derivatives pricing
not_for: Spot cryptocurrency trading (Deribit is derivatives-only — use Binance for spot)
Retail traders without derivatives/options experience (high risk, complex instruments)
US users (Deribit withdrew from US market — use CME or LedgerX for regulated US crypto options)
best_when: A professional options trader or market maker needs the deepest crypto options liquidity — Deribit is the dominant venue for BTC and ETH options globally (80%+ market share).
avoid_when: You need spot trading, are in the US, lack derivatives expertise, or need regulated futures reporting.

auth.notes: Client credentials: client_id (API key) + client_secret (API secret). Authentication via public/auth method (JSON-RPC). Returns access_token (1-hour expiry) and refresh_token. Bearer token in Authorization header or JSON-RPC params. WebSocket is primary interface — REST also available. API key scopes: account:read, account:read_write, trade:read, trade:read_write, wallet:read, wallet:read_write, block_trade:read, block_trade:read_write. No OAuth2 (custom token endpoint).

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access free; maker -0.01% (rebate) / taker 0.03% for options
pricing.paid_tiers: Volume-based fee tiers; institutional rates available
pricing.notes: Panama incorporated. Founded 2016 (John Jansen, Amsterdam). 80%+ of global crypto options volume. Testnet available (test.deribit.com) for development. BTC and ETH derivatives (options, perps, futures). Options: American and European style. Expiries: daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly. Settlement: cash-settled in crypto (DVOL index for ETH). Open interest tracking. Options analytics built-in: greeks, implied volatility. Block trades for large institutional orders. Withdrew from US market (unregistered exchange). 24/7 trading. Portfolio margining. Insurance fund. Became subsidiary of Nasdaq-listed company Abcam in 2022? (unconfirmed — verify current ownership).

performance.rate_limits: 20 requests/second for private methods; 500/second for public; WebSocket preferred

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON-RPC primary (not REST). Custom auth with access_token (1-hour). Testnet available. US blocked. Panama. 80%+ crypto options market share. Options greeks built-in. WebSocket preferred over REST.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Order reads idempotent by order ID. Access token must be refreshed after 1 hour. Trade reads idempotent by trade ID. Order creation not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: JSON-RPC over WebSocket is primary interface — REST is secondary and less feature-complete
Custom auth: call auth method → receive access_token (1-hour) + refresh_token → use Bearer token
access_token expires in 1 hour — implement refresh logic using refresh_token
Testnet at test.deribit.com — create separate API keys for testnet (different from mainnet)
US users blocked since 2020 — verify jurisdiction compliance
Instrument naming: BTC-PERPETUAL, BTC-31MAR23, BTC-31MAR23-25000-C (option format)

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Custom OAuth-like token flow (1-hour access tokens). Fine-grained API key scopes. Testnet for safe development. Panama jurisdiction (limited regulation). US market withdrawn. Cryptocurrency derivatives — high complexity and high value target. JSON-RPC over WebSocket (secure channel). Portfolio margining increases liquidation risk management complexity.
